source     = ByteDance-Seed/Bagel  (https://github.com/ByteDance-Seed/Bagel)
commit     = a2fa77dd8caeefc41e6607ae0ec17408d3f4ee9f
short      = a2fa77d
commit_msg = Merge pull request #299 from Purshow/fix-wise-eval
commit_date= 2026-05-05 01:01:02 +0800
vendored   = 2026-06-06  (into unirl/models/bagel/vendor/)
target     = UniRL-main (haonan3/UniRL main @ e799ff29)

Migration: docs/BAGEL_MIGRATION_PLAN.md  (replace flow_grpo-vendored fork with
pristine official modeling + external rl_ops.py).

Mechanical changes (see vendor/__init__.py):
  - import-root rewrite modeling./data./inferencer -> unirl.models.bagel.vendor.*
  - added modeling/cache_utils/__init__.py
  - data/ subset only (data_utils.py + transforms.py + __init__.py)
Plus local compatibility edits (NOT pristine):
  1. Grad-safety edit: modeling/bagel/qwen2_navit.py
  PackedAttention gen-mode output writes into a fresh torch.zeros_like tensor
  instead of inplace-indexing the flash_attn view (autograd forbids inplace on a
  custom-Function view during RL replay backprop). Math unchanged. Marked inline.
  2. Transformers 5 config compatibility:
     modeling/qwen2/modeling_qwen2.py and modeling/bagel/qwen2_navit.py use
     getattr(config, "pad_token_id", None) to preserve transformers 4 behavior
     for BAGEL's llm_config.json, which omits pad_token_id.
     modeling/qwen2/modeling_qwen2.py also preserves the default RoPE init path
     because transformers 5's ROPE_INIT_FUNCTIONS no longer exposes "default".
  3. Reference-inferencer VAE decode dtype compatibility:
     inferencer.py temporarily decodes with the VAE in fp32, then restores its
     loaded dtype. Unlike BagelVAEDecodeStage's sticky-fp32 pipeline path, the
     standalone inferencer encodes through the VAE directly and does not cast
     encode I/O at the boundary.

To re-vendor a newer upstream:
  1. git -C Bagel fetch && git -C Bagel checkout <new_commit>
  2. recopy modeling/ + inferencer.py + data/{data_utils,transforms,__init__}.py
  3. re-apply the 9 import-root rewrites + cache_utils/__init__.py
  4. re-apply the documented local compatibility edits
  5. bump this file; re-run the Phase 2 ratio==1 test AND a replay-backward/training
     smoke before trusting (the ratio test alone runs under no_grad and won't catch
     grad-only issues like the inplace-on-view above).
